explanation:
Building and renovation projects require precise technical calculations: concrete, steel, bricks, finishing coatings, waterproofing. Industry standards guide best practices.

## How It's Calculated

Pump power calculation determines the wattage required to elevate water based on three key factors: flow rate (liters per minute), elevation height (meters), and pump efficiency (percentage). This calculator applies the standard engineering formula with your input values, checking for typical ranges and alerting when values fall outside reasonable parameters.

## Final Notes

This calculation is for reference and guidance only. For critical decisions (financial, technical, or design-related), consult with a licensed professional. Data is current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What size pump do I need for my water system?
faq.a: Use this calculator with your flow rate requirement, total elevation height (suction lift + discharge), and expected pump efficiency to find the required watts and horsepower.

faq.q: What's the difference between submersible and surface pumps?
faq.a: Submersible pumps work best for depths greater than 8 meters. Surface pumps are suitable for shallow lifts and lower elevation requirements.

faq.q: What flow rate do I need for a household?
faq.a: A typical 4-person home requires 30–60 L/min. Larger properties or agricultural uses typically need 60–200 L/min.

faq.q: What are standard commercial pump sizes available?
faq.a: Common pump sizes are ½, ¾, 1, 1.5, 2, and 3 HP. Always choose the next larger size above your calculated requirement.

faq.q: How do I calculate total pumping height?
faq.a: Add the suction lift (depth from water source to pump) plus the discharge head (height to your tank or endpoint). This total is your elevation height (H).

faq.q: What does pump efficiency mean and how does it affect power?
faq.a: Efficiency is the percentage of input power converted to useful work. Typical values range 60–85%. Lower efficiency requires more watts to achieve the same flow and height.

faq.q: Can I automate my pump to run only when needed?
faq.a: Yes. Install a pressure switch and tank-mounted float valve to prevent unnecessary runtime and extend pump life.

faq.q: Do you collect or store my data?
faq.a: No. All calculations run in your browser. We do not send data to any server.
